title: fritillaria tubaeformis subsp. tubaeformis
intro: Fritillaria tubaeformis subsp. tubaeformis, also known as the Sierra Nevada fritillaria or Sierra Nevada lily, is a rare, bulbous perennial plant native to the Sierra Nevada mountain range in California and Nevada. This plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Fritillaria tubaeformis subsp. tubaeformis include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of the common cold. Fritillaria tubaeformis subsp. tubaeformis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
